title: centaurea jacea subsp. schrankii
intro: Centaurea jacea subsp. schrankii, also known as rough knapweed, is a subspecies of the knapweed plant, characterized by its prickly leaves and purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Centaurea jacea subsp. schrankii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing digestive problems, as well as its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Centaurea jacea subsp. schrankii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the plant's flowers and leaves.
